title: Melted Buttery Saudi Arabian Bulgur Wheat Pilaf
description: A one-pot, plant-based-friendly Saudi Arabian dish featuring bulgur wheat, this recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner, it is buttery and has a snappy texture.
instructions: Rinse the bulgur wheat in cold water, then drain and set aside.
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a large fondue pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to brown.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the cardamom powder and cumin powder, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the bulgur wheat, water, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the pot.
Simmer for 15 minutes, or until the water has been absorbed and the bulgur wheat is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter until melted and well combined.
